summary:
Claim: Steroids delay tissue repair by impairing collagen synthesis and fibroblast function.  
Evidence: 1) Corticosteroids inhibit fibroblast proliferation and collagen production. 2) Clinical studies show delayed wound healing in patients using long-term corticosteroid therapy.  
Answer: A ✅  

(One sentence claim, two bullet points for evidence, then clear selection.)

Final output strictly follows format.
Claim: Steroids delay tissue repair by impairing collagen synthesis and fibroblast function.  
Evidence: - Corticosteroids inhibit fibroblast proliferation and collagen production. - Clinical studies demonstrate delayed wound healing in patients receiving prolonged glucocorticoid treatment.  
Answer: A ✅

Claim: Steroids delay tissue repair by impairing collagen synthesis and fibroblast function.  
Evidence: - Corticosteroids inhibit fibroblast proliferation and collagen production. - Clinical studies demonstrate delayed wound healing in patients receiving prolonged glucocorticoid treatment.  
Answer: A ✅
